rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Petition Of Francis X. Browne
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondent Rosie M. Thompson moves to dismiss Petitioner Francis X. Browne's petition for an order to confirm validity of trust, confirm assets to revocable trust and distribut[e] gifts, filed under Probate Code sections 17200 and 850 ("Petition"). Respondent's motion is DENIED. Probate Code section 17202 does not compel dismissal. The Petition is "reasonably necessary" to protect Browne's interest, in that it is principally directed at relief not sought in the decedent's children's petition (i.e., confirmation of the 2010 Bentley Continental Speed as a trust asset and distribution of the Bentley to Browne). It is easy to imagine decedent's children's petition being resolved without resolution of either of the issues at the heart of Browne's petition. Moreover, the court declines to exercise its discretion to dismiss the Petition. Separately, the court finds that Mr. Browne has standing to bring the petition under sections 17200 and 850. Under the verified allegations of the petition, the trust exists, the Bentley is a trust asset and under the trust Browne is due the Bentley. Notably, this is not a case where a former beneficiary seeks to override the known and clear terms of the current trust instrument that provide that petitioner is neither a trustee nor a beneficiary. (See Barefoot v. Jennings (2019) 27 Cal.App.5th 1, 5-8, rev. granted Dec. 12, 2019.) The Petition is cognizable under section 850 because the verified allegations demonstrate that Browne is a beneficiary or interested party. (See Prob. Code, §850, subd. (a)(3).) Petitioner Browne shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on Tuesday, December 17, 2019.
